evidence.scientific_clarity: Grouped bar chart clearly shows OursModel winning all 5 benchmarks; stars mark per-benchmark top performer; figure is readable at single-column 3.3in width.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype=none both set correctly; font family is 'sans-serif' but resolves to default DejaVu Sans (not a polished publication font like Helvetica/Arial); sizes 6-8pt are small but functional.
evidence.palette: Colorblind-safe 4-color palette with hatches for B&W; however green (#4dac26) and red (#ca0020) are used for categorical methods, violating the directional-reserved convention stated in the brief.
evidence.layout_density: y-range trimmed to data (brief hint honored: does not start at 0 when values are high); tight_layout used; no overlapping labels or gaping whitespace; bar spacing is compact and appropriate.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption fully matches figure content; legend placed inside at lower-right without obscuring data; stars explained in caption; all methods and benchmarks named.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF confirmed as real vector (version 1.4); pdffonts shows CID TrueType font embedded with fonttype=42; text is selectable.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (39KB), figure.png (70KB), figure.svg, caption.tex (531B), gen_figure.py (3.3KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states specific finding ('OursModel achieves the highest accuracy on all five tasks'), describes error bars (5 seeds), explains star markers and hatching convention, and names all methods and benchmarks.

overall_strengths: Complete file set with correct vector settings (pdf.fonttype=42, svg.fonttype=none) and real embedded TrueType fonts in the PDF.
Caption is thorough and publication-ready: specific numerical claim, error bar description, accessibility note (hatching), and per-benchmark star annotations all consistent with the figure.
overall_weaknesses: Default DejaVu Sans font used instead of a publication-quality sans-serif (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or Liberation Sans); font sizes (6-8pt) are at the lower edge for readability.
Green (#4dac26) and red (#ca0020) are assigned to categorical methods (Method-A and OursModel) rather than being reserved for directional semantics, violating the brief's palette discipline hint.
